subject: Re: Lily's birthday party plans
body: Derek — sounds like so much fun! I can absolutely be there that Saturday. Would it be helpful if I brought my deviled eggs and the lemon pound cake? Lily is going to love the little garden theme you mentioned. Let me know what time you need me to arrive so I can help with setup.\n\nMom

subject: Thinking of you — hope the new job is going well
body:
Megan sweetheart,

I know we talked Sunday but I just wanted to drop a note and say I'm so proud of you. Starting a new position is nerve-wracking and exciting all at once. If you ever need to talk through anything — work stuff or just life — you know I'm always here.

I'll have a pot of vegetable soup ready when you come visit in November.

All my love,
Mom

subject: Request: Schedule adjustment week of November 10
body:
Donna,

I wanted to reach out about the week of November 10. I have a follow-up with Dr. Rajan on the 11th at 2:30 and I was hoping I could swap my Wednesday shift that week with someone. I've already spoken with Linda and she's willing to trade if that works for you. Just need your sign-off.

Thank you,
Lauren Chapman, RN

subject: Fabric availability question — Moda Bella Solids
body:
Hello Eleanor,

I'm working on a double wedding ring quilt and I'm looking for about 3 yards of the Moda Bella Solids in the dusty rose — colorway 9900-309 if I have the number right. Do you have that in stock or would I need to order it? Also curious whether you're carrying the new Kaufman crosshatch in sage this season.

Thank you so much,
Lauren Chapman

subject: Blowing Rock trip — cabin details
body:
Patsy!

I finally looked up the cabin rental and I think the Laurel Creek one on the ridge is perfect — two bedrooms, a porch with rocking chairs, and it's only about 10 minutes from downtown. Rates look reasonable for mid-October. Should we split it 50/50?

Also — do you want to drive up together or meet there? I'm thinking we leave early on the 12th to beat the leaf-peeping traffic.

Can't wait!
Lauren

subject: Re: Choir cantata music for Advent
body:
Carol,

I looked through the score and I think the alto line in the third movement is going to be a challenge for some of us — there's a sustained high G that I know I struggle with. Would you be able to work with the altos separately before the full choir rehearsal in October? Even one extra session would help.

Also, I can bring the copies to Thursday's practice if you want to distribute them then.

Lauren

subject: Re: Question about the rapid response protocol
body:
Renee,

Good question. The rapid response criteria we use on this floor are based on any single-system deterioration — RR above 25, SpO2 dropping below 92% on room air, or altered mentation that's new. Don't wait for multiple signs. Call early. The team would rather come and find things are okay than miss a window.

You're doing well. Keep asking questions — that's exactly what good nurses do.

Lauren

subject: Book club — December pick
body:
Sandra,

I finished Demon Copperhead last week and I think it would make for a wonderful December discussion — there's so much to talk about regarding community and healthcare in rural Appalachia. Would you float it to the group? I can host if we do the second Thursday.

Lauren

subject: Volunteer — Meals Ministry January
body:
Pastor Whitley,

I wanted to sign up to help with the Meals Ministry on the third Saturday of January. I know Patsy is already on the list and we could coordinate together. Would you pass along the contact for whoever is coordinating that month?

Thank you,
Lauren Chapman

subject: Re: Quilting circle — November meeting date
body:
Diane,

The second Saturday in November works perfectly for me. I'll bring the double wedding ring blocks I've been working on — they're finally starting to come together. Should I also bring the rotary cutter set I mentioned? I think two or three of us could use the extra.

See you then!
Lauren

subject: Lunch Thursday?
body:
Linda,

I know you're off Thursday too — any chance you want to grab lunch at that new sandwich place on Providence? I need to do my grocery run anyway so I thought we could make a day of it before choir practice.

Let me know,
Lauren

subject: Re: Water aerobics — summer schedule
body:
Gail,

Thank goodness the 11 AM class is staying on Saturdays — I was worried when I saw the notice on the board. Did you sign up for the new deep-water session too? I was thinking about it. My knees have been better since we started and I don't want to break the streak.

See you Saturday!
Lauren

subject: Re: Charting question — I&O documentation
body:
Marcus,

For strict I&O, document every output every hour for ICU transfers and every four hours for telemetry patients unless the physician orders otherwise. Make sure you're recording chest tube drainage separately from Foley — the system has separate fields and it matters for the fluid balance calculation.

Ask me again at handoff if you're still unsure. Happy to walk through it.

Lauren

subject: Recipes for Lily — soft foods she might like
body:
Derek,

Here are a few things that worked well when you were little and that I think Lily might enjoy now that she's getting more adventurous:

- Mashed sweet potato with a tiny bit of butter and cinnamon
- Soft scrambled eggs with mild shredded cheese
- Banana slices with peanut butter thinned with a little water

I can type up the full sweet potato casserole recipe too if you want it for Thanksgiving planning.

Love,
Mom

subject: Bed 12 — night handoff notes
body:
Tom,

Quick heads up on Bed 12 before you take over. Mrs. Holloway came back from CT at 1715 — radiology called with prelim read, attending is aware. She's been anxious about the results and her daughter is asking questions. I'd suggest having someone check in on her around 2000. IV site looks good, no infiltration.

Call my cell if anything looks off overnight.

Lauren

subject: Re: Recommendations for RA flare
body: Patsy — you know I always run my own questions through you before I call Dr. Rajan's office. The morning stiffness has been running about 90 minutes the last two weeks which is longer than usual. I did increase the ibuprofen a little. Think it's worth calling or wait for my October appointment? Honest opinion, friend to friend.\n\nLauren

subject: PTO request — October 12-16
body:
Submitting PTO request for October 12-16 (Monday through Friday). I have a previously scheduled cabin trip with a friend and would appreciate having those days approved. I have worked the last three third-weekend rotations without a swap. Thank you for considering.

Lauren Chapman, RN
Charge Nurse, 4 West

subject: Re: Photos from Lily's spring portraits
body:
Jenna, these are absolutely precious. The one with her in the sunflower field — I want to have that framed. Could you send me the full resolution file? I was thinking an 8x10 for the hallway and maybe a smaller one for my desk at work.

You and Derek did such a good job with her. She's the light of my life.

Love,
Lauren

subject: Garden update + tomato question
body:
Megan,

The Cherokee Purples are finally setting fruit — I counted seven little green tomatoes this morning. I do have a question though. One of the Better Boys has some yellowing on the lower leaves. I don't think it's blight because the spots aren't water-soaked but I'm not sure. I'll send a photo next time I'm out there. Did you have this issue with the plants you put in on your balcony?

Mom

subject: Sunday soloist schedule — second half of year
body:
Carol and Pastor Whitley,

I'm putting together the soloist rotation for November through December. So far I have Beverly for two Sundays in December and Thomas for the first Sunday in November. Could each of you let me know your availability and any dates that are already spoken for? I want to make sure we're covered for Advent especially.

Thank you,
Lauren
